original_tool_input.base_case: Over the next 30-60 days the base case is a choppy stabilization-to-recovery attempt off the 50-day SMA. The bull skeleton remains mechanically intact (50d > 200d, price ~8% above the 200d), and the active correction has now retraced fully into the 38.2-61.8% Fib zone where the rising 50-day SMA (~7,248) is acting as primary support. RSI and MFI are turning up from neutral/depressed readings and VIX has reset back below 18, which supports a constructive base-building bias — but ERP at a valuation ceiling, weak breadth, and a still-elevated junk spread cap upside and keep this a recovery-watch rather than a fresh trend leg.
original_tool_input.trend_regime: 50-day SMA (~7,248) remains above the rising 200-day SMA (~6,882) — mechanically healthy bullish regime, but maturing after a confirmed distribution top in early June. Price sits ~2.5% above the 50d and ~8.0% above the 200d, having bounced off the 50d after a multi-week pullback from the 7,621 swing high.
original_tool_input.valuation_bucket: Forward PE 20.09x (20-22x cautious-buying band); ERP 0.49% sits in the 0.0-0.5% valuation-ceiling band — trim bias, no aggressive adds.

signals.bollinger_position: Mid-to-upper band, recovering toward middle band from below
signals.middle_band_regime: Reclaimed 20-day SMA after breakdown — short-term structure improving
signals.vix_regime_detail: VIX 17.68, back below 20 and below its 50-day MA

what_changed_today: SPX closed 7,431.46 (+) in the top third of the daily intraday range (low 7,363 / high 7,456) — first clean strong close after a multi-session distribution sequence.
VIX collapsed from ~22 to 17.68, dropping back below the 20 risk threshold and below its 50-day MA — the volatility-driven 'MA less reliable' caveat is lifted.
RSI-14 turned up to mid-50s and MFI surged toward ~62 on the 5-day chart — short-term momentum recovering from the correction low; price has reclaimed the 20-day SMA.
Price bounced off and held the rising 50-day SMA (~7,248) and now sits at the 61.8% Fib (7,443) zone — the active leg is recovering rather than cascading.
Monte Carlo edge remains decisively down-first (70% raw) because the engine re-anchored the downside straddle to the liquidation-caution level 7,392 just below current price.
original_tool_input.narrative_summary: The market is Late Bull / Topping but staging a credible recovery: after an early-June distribution top and a pullback to the rising 50-day SMA, price closed strong in the top third of its range, reclaimed the 20-day SMA, and saw VIX reset back below 18 while RSI/MFI turned up. That technical repair is genuine and lifts the posture off pure defense. But the structural ceiling is unchanged — ERP at just 0.49% (valuation ceiling), McClellan breadth in Extreme Fear, junk spreads elevated, and a Monte Carlo edge still pinned down-first because the downside target sits right beneath spot. This is a stabilization-and-watch tape: the bounce earns partial re-entry confirmation, but the valuation ceiling and weak internals forbid aggressive adds.
open_questions: Does the recovery follow through and reclaim the 38.2% Fib (7,511) and the prior 7,621 swing high, or does it stall at the 61.8%/20-day-SMA zone and roll over again?
Can breadth (McClellan ~990, Extreme Fear) confirm the price recovery, or does the persistent breadth/credit divergence cap any rally as narrow leadership?
With the Monte Carlo downside target re-anchored to 7,392 just below spot, is a brief retest of that level the launch point for a cleaner re-entry, or a break toward the nervous zone at 7,240?
original_tool_input.primary_tension: A genuine short-term technical recovery — strong top-of-range close, reclaimed 20-day SMA, VIX back under 18, RSI/MFI turning up off the 50-day SMA — is colliding with an unchanged structural ceiling: ERP at the 0.49% valuation cap, Extreme Fear breadth, an elevated junk spread, and a Monte Carlo edge still 70% down-first because the downside target sits just beneath spot. The result is a constructive-but-capped tape that supports partial re-entry confirmation while forbidding aggressive adds.
confirming_evidence: Trend skeleton intact: 50-day SMA (~7,248) above rising 200-day SMA (~6,882); price ~8% above the 200d (6-month/1-year charts).
Strong intraday close in the top third of the daily range (7,431 vs range 7,363-7,456) after a multi-day distribution sequence (01_spx_intraday.png).
RSI-14 turning up to mid-50s and MFI rising toward ~62 on the 5-day panel — short-term momentum recovery off the correction low (02_spx_5day.png).
VIX reset to 17.68, back below 20 and below its 50-day MA — volatility regime normalized, MA support more reliable (13_vix_volatility.png).
Price held and bounced off the rising 50-day SMA and reclaimed the 20-day SMA — short-term structure repaired (03_spx_1month.png).

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Price recovering off the 50-day SMA with momentum turning up and VIX normalized supports a constructive base.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: ERP at just 0.49% sits in the 0.0-0.5% valuation-ceiling band — trim bias, no aggressive adds; the re-entry floor (7,419) is essentially at spot, so there is no valuation cushion underneath the bounce.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: ERP 0.0-0.5% = valuation ceiling, trim bias, no aggressive adds.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Index price is recovering with a strong close and reclaimed 20-day SMA.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: McClellan Summation in Extreme Fear (~990) and junk spread still elevated (~1.35%) — breadth and credit have not confirmed the price recovery, signaling narrow leadership.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: When breadth and credit both diverge from price, elevate caution even if price has not broken down.

conflicting_evidence.bullish_read: Technical signals (close, RSI/MFI, VIX, 20-day reclaim) point to recovery.
conflicting_evidence.bearish_read: Monte Carlo is 70% down-first because the re-anchored downside target (7,392) sits just below spot; the edge does not meet any regime threshold for bullish action.
conflicting_evidence.framework_rule: Never use Monte Carlo in isolation; if adjusted probability does not meet the regime threshold, the setup is not actionable.
